title: Bridge Essay: Volition and control in law and in brain science: neurolegal translation of a foundational concept.
concept: Sense of agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nested agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.
**Phase context:** Sense of agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.

**Volition and control in law and in brain science: neurolegal translation of a foundational concept.** — Front Hum Neurosci (2024). DOI: 10.3389/fnhum.2024.1401895. PMID: 39290567. The law assumes that healthy adults are generally responsible for their actions and have the ability to control their behavior based on rational and moral principles. This contrasts with some recent neuroscientific accounts of action control. Nevertheless, both law and neuroscience acknowledge that strong emotions including fear and anger may "trigger" loss of normal voluntary control over action.
